title: Invoice Operations Agent with HITL
problem: Finance teams receive hundreds of invoices by email daily. Manual data entry into ERP systems is error-prone, slow, and a bottleneck for month-end close.
solution: Email-triggered agent extracts invoice fields, validates against PO data, flags anomalies for human approval, then writes approved invoices to the ERP system — with full audit trail.
workflowSteps: Email trigger fires on new invoice receipt
Document extraction LLM parses vendor, amount, line items, PO reference
Validation: cross-checks PO system for matching purchase order
If anomaly detected (amount mismatch, unknown vendor): pause → HITL approval gate
On approval: writes entry to ERP (NetSuite, SAP), archives document, sends confirmation
keyInsights: The durable workflow pattern is essential here — a mid-run crash must not double-post an ERP entry. Idempotency keys on the ERP write activity prevent duplicate postings under retries.

relatedExams.why: Deploy AI-Powered Business Solutions — ERP integration and workflow automation
Agents and Workflows — durable execution, HITL

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([Email Trigger]) --> B[Extract Invoice Fields]
  B --> C{Matches PO?}
  C -->|Yes| D[Auto-Approve]
  C -->|Anomaly| E[HITL Review Gate]
  E -->|Approved| D
  E -->|Rejected| F([Flag and Escalate])
  D --> G([ERP Write + Idempotency Key])
architectureNotes: The agent extracts structured fields (vendor, amount, line items) from raw invoice PDFs using an extraction model, then compares them against PO records. Matched invoices are auto-approved and written to the ERP with an idempotency key. Mismatches or low-confidence extractions are routed to a human reviewer via a structured task queue. The HITL gate prevents erroneous payments while keeping auto-approval rates high for routine invoices.

failureModes.mitigation: Confidence threshold gate — route to HITL if extraction score < 0.85; store raw OCR alongside structured output for audit.

failureModes.mode: ERP write failure causes duplicate posting on retry
failureModes.mitigation: Idempotency key (invoice_id + fiscal_period) stored in Redis; ERP adapter checks key before posting and returns 200 if already applied.

failureModes.mode: HITL queue backlog during month-end processing
failureModes.mitigation: Priority queue with SLA timer; auto-escalate to manager after 4 hours; route high-value invoices to dedicated reviewers.
Circuit breaker with fallback to cached PO snapshot (TTL 1h); mark invoice as 'pending PO validation' and retry async.

scalingConsiderations: Use async email-to-queue fanout (SQS/Service Bus) to decouple ingestion from processing — handles burst at month-end without dropping invoices.
Batch extraction for PDF-to-structured jobs; single-pass models (Form Recognizer) are 3–5× cheaper than multi-turn LLM extraction for standard templates.
HITL queue capacity: plan for 15–20% manual review rate on first deployment; drops to 3–5% after 90 days of fine-tuning on company-specific invoice formats.
ERP write throughput: apply back-pressure — max 50 concurrent ERP writes to avoid lock contention on the GL tables.

integrations.note: Microsoft Exchange / Gmail via Graph API or IMAP listener
BAPI or REST API for GL posting; must be idempotent
Validates invoice against open purchase orders
Archives raw PDF + structured JSON for audit
Routes exceptions to human reviewers with full context
